name: Holy Ghost Parish
description_short: Holy Ghost Parish is a Catholic religious institution in Whitman, MA offering sacraments, mass, and religious education—not a consumer finance company.
description_long:
Holy Ghost Parish is a Roman Catholic parish located at 518 Washington Street in Whitman, Massachusetts. It operates as a spiritual community under the leadership of the Church, providing traditional Catholic services and programs to its congregation and local community. The parish has been misclassified in the payday-alternatives category, which is factually incorrect based on available information.

The parish offers standard Catholic religious services including weekly masses (Saturday vigil at 4:00pm and Sunday masses at 7:30am and 10:30am), weekday masses on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday at 9:00am, sacraments, and religious education programs. The parish also operates St. Bridget School, maintains a perpetual adoration chapel, and provides parishioner registration and bulletin services. These are exclusively religious and educational offerings.

Holy Ghost Parish distinguishes itself as a faith-based community institution rather than a financial services provider. It focuses on spiritual formation, liturgical worship, and Catholic education through its affiliated school. The parish is part of the larger Catholic Church structure and operates within that framework.

Importantly, there is no evidence from the website that Holy Ghost Parish offers any consumer finance products, services, or alternatives. It does not provide payday loans, payday alternatives, credit services, personal loans, emergency cash, or any financial products whatsoever. The categorization as a payday-alternatives lender is entirely inaccurate and appears to be a data error.

This listing appears to be a data error and requires immediate recategorization or removal from a consumer finance platform.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
